pages.title: About | Chris Kane MP
pages.meta_description: Chris Kane is the Member of Parliament for Stirling and Strathallan. Chris is a Labour politician, first elected to parliament in...
pages.text_excerpt: About | Chris Kane MP >>>>>>>>> (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) >>>>>>>>> End (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) Find me on Facebook 01786 652345 Email Us HOME ABOUT NEWS NEWSLETTER CONTACT Online Form Coffee Mornings Visit Parliament Event Request Useful Links SUPERSTARS OPPORTUNITIES ABOUT Find out more about my work, campaigns and how I can help you with local issues and concerns. Chris Kane is the Member of Parliament for Stirling and Strathallan. Chris is a Labour politician, first elected to parliament in July 2024. From 2017-2024, Chris was a local authority councillor in Stirling (Stirling East ward), where he held roles including Leader of the Council (2022-2024), Deputy Leader of the Council (2020-2022) and Convenor of the Community Planning and Regeneration Committee (2017-2022). Chris has served on the Boards of organisations including Loch Lomond and the Trossachs National Park and the Stirling Smith Art Gallery and Museum. His route into politics was through community activities; he was the Chair of his local community council (Braehead and District) and established a local Community Development Trust, where he raised over £300K to build a 10,000 square metre Community Garden which was named the ‘best community space in the UK’ by the Society of Garden Designers in 2015. Chris was born in Stirling. He lives there with his wife, Anne, and their two teenage children. Chris attended Stirling High School and Stirling University, graduating with a BA in Media Studies and a Postgraduate Diploma in Entrepreneurial Studies. Away from politics, Chris was a full-time broadcaster and journalist for many years, working with most of Scotland’s radio stations and a few of Scotland’s local newspapers. In recent years he worked as a marketing and communications consultant and created digital content (website blogs, podcasts, press releases, etc) for businesses and organisations. pages.title: Chris Raises Heating Oil Price Surge in Parliament to Support Rural Scots - Chris Kane MP | Stirling and Strathallan
pages.h1: Chris Raises Heating Oil Price Surge in Parliament to Support Rural Scots
pages.meta_description: Chris has raised urgent questions in Parliament following the sharp rise in heating oil prices affecting rural and off‑gas‑grid households across Scotland, due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
pages.text_excerpt: Chris Raises Heating Oil Price Surge in Parliament to Support Rural Scots - Chris Kane MP | Stirling and Strathallan >>>>>>>>> (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) >>>>>>>>> End (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) Find me on Facebook 01786 652345 Email Us HOME ABOUT NEWS NEWSLETTER CONTACT Online Form Coffee Mornings Visit Parliament Event Request Useful Links SUPERSTARS OPPORTUNITIES Home » Chris Raises Heating Oil Price Surge in Parliament to Support Rural Scots Chris Raises Heating Oil Price Surge in Parliament to Support Rural Scots Share on Social Media Chris has raised urgent questions in Parliament following the sharp rise in heating oil prices affecting rural and off‑gas‑grid households across Scotland. Global events have pushed heating oil prices sharply upwards in recent days, with households that rely on oil seeing some of the steepest increases. While only around 5% of Scottish households use heating oil as their main heating source, the impact is most deeply felt in rural communities, including many across Stirling and Strathallan, where homes are not connected to the gas network and have no protection from Ofgem’s price cap. Chris said: “Many rural households across Scotland depend entirely on heating oil, and recent price increases are causing real anxiety. I’m asking the Government to look again at what additional support can be offered, and to work closely with the Scottish Government so that rural and off‑grid communities are not left behind.” He added: “This is a fast‑moving situation, and I want to see solutions that give people confidence and stability. I’ll keep working constructively with Ministers to ensure households in areas like Stirling and Strathallan receive the support they need.” Chris has submitted a series of parliamentary questions to the UK Government, seeking clarity on what support is available now, what assessments have been made of fuel poverty in rural areas, and how the UK and Scottish governments can work together to protect off‑grid households. He confirmed he will continue to monitor the situation closely and support affected constituents as prices evolve. If you are affected by rising prices, click here to share your views. pages.title: Chris Visits Killin Following Water Supply Issues - Chris Kane MP | Stirling and Strathallan
pages.h1: Chris Visits Killin Following Water Supply Issues
pages.meta_description: Yesterday, Chris met with representatives from SEPA, Scottish Water, and residents in Killin to discuss the ongoing situation with the village’s water supply.
pages.text_excerpt: Chris Visits Killin Following Water Supply Issues - Chris Kane MP | Stirling and Strathallan >>>>>>>>> (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) >>>>>>>>> End (Gropup 1 - Necessary Cookies) Find me on Facebook 01786 652345 Email Us HOME ABOUT NEWS NEWSLETTER CONTACT Online Form Coffee Mornings Visit Parliament Event Request Useful Links SUPERSTARS OPPORTUNITIES Home » Chris Visits Killin Following Water Supply Issues Chris Visits Killin Following Water Supply Issues Share on Social Media Yesterday, Chris met with representatives from SEPA, Scottish Water, and residents in Killin to discuss the ongoing situation with the village’s water supply. He posted the following update on Social Media: I’m pleased that the water has now been declared safe for consumption, allowing residents to use their tap water as normal again. Residents told me they were impressed with Scottish Water’s response, particularly the swift provision of bottled water and regular updates. Scottish Water went above and beyond, including: 💧54 pallets of bottled water supplied (around 52,000 litres) 👥 100+ Scottish Water volunteers redeployed from across the country, including Inverclyde, Lanarkshire, Fife and Glasgow 🏢 200+ visits to the customer information point at McLaren Hall car park 🍔 1,000+ hot meals served from their food van Scottish Water, in turn, praised the community for its cooperation, which helped them carry out their work more effectively. SEPA confirmed they were notified of the diesel spillage on Thursday evening and have had officers on site since Friday. Their investigation into the cause and impact is ongoing. I also conveyed feedback from residents on how SEPA’s communications could be improved. I am pleased that they are committed to providing daily updates as the situation progresses. I will continue to push for updates as the investigation unfolds. Scottish Water Van at McLaren Hall, Killin Share on Social Media KEEP UPDATED SUBSCRIBE FOR EMAIL UPDATES Keep Updated Subscribe to my email newsletter to keep updated on my news, campaigns and work in the local area.
